generated_oml:
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting system controlled via LightTwinComponent commands validated for brightness (0-100%) and color temperature (Kelvin) via Ditto's policy-based validation"
]

instance BuildingSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "AECO domain building with Building Control System components including sensors, actuators, and controllers"
    base:contains LightingActuator
]

instance DTFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized DT Framework acting as hub for bidirectional data exchange"
]

instance BuildingControlEngineer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role for device modeling in Building Control System"
]

instance FacilityManager : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role for real-time monitoring and control of building operations"
]

instance DTEngineer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Role for service integration and extension via modular extensions"
]

instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Centralized architecture with DT Framework as hub for bidirectional data exchange"
    base:contains DTFramework, BuildingControlEngineer, FacilityManager, DTEngineer
]

instance MDEFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework for transforming BIM models into DT artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicConfigurationService, CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono for at-least-once message delivery of sensor and actuator data"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto for policy-based validation of value ranges/units and persistent event storage"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlLogicConfigurationService
]

instance UE5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 for interactive visualization and real-time synchronization between physical and digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance TwinLightProject : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Research project supporting modular architecture for DT services"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

instance BOREALISProject : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Research project supporting real-time data exchange capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance BIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Building Information Model enriched with Revit photometric values for lighting"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEFramework
]

instance LightTwinComponent : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for light control implementing TwinComponent extension"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEFramework
]

instance LightFeatureParser : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "FeatureParser extension for processing brightness and color temperature data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEFramework
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time monitoring for Facility Managers"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive visualization for Facility Managers"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for infrastructure optimization via predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight
]

instance ControlLogicConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for configuring control logic by Building Control Engineers"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlConfigurationAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for custom integration via modular extensions for DT Engineers"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomServiceExtensionAction
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring insight for building operations"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization insight for building operations"
]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics insight for infrastructure optimization"
]

instance ControlConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Control logic configuration action for Building Control Engineers"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CustomServiceExtensionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Custom service integration action for DT Engineers"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands issued by Facility Managers via interactive visualization interfaces enable bidirectional data exchange for real-time control actions; however, command types, message formats, validation mechanisms, feedback systems, conflict resolution, and execution confirmation details are not specified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Modular architecture with standardized extension points for integrating custom services and analytics, runtime configuration management enabling adjustments without full redeployment, and a central DT Framework facilitating holistic building lifecycle management through scalable and interoperable component integration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The framework supports the complete DT lifecycle from initial device modeling within BIM environments (defining sensors, actuators, controllers), through Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) for structured representation extraction and automatic artifact generation, real-time data synchronization for operational monitoring and control, and modular evolution via custom services integrated by DT Engineers. Stakeholder roles (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) are defined for respective lifecycle phases, but governance approaches are not explicitly detailed."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured through accurate rendering of BIM metadata (e.g., Revit photometric values for lighting in UE5 scene rendering). Validation includes Eclipse Ditto's policy-based checks for sensor data ranges/units and pre-execution validation of actuator commands to prevent unsafe operations. Fault tolerance mechanisms—Eclipse Hono's at-least-once message delivery and Ditto's persistent event storage—maintain data integrity and resilience against network failures, ensuring synchronization and operational safety between physical assets and the Digital Twin."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Technical infrastructure utilizes Eclipse Hono and Ditto for bidirectional communication; however, network topology, bandwidth, latency requirements, redundancy mechanisms, and security protocols are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment leverages a modular architecture with automated processes for reproducible setups and runtime configuration management to support scalability and adaptability; however, specific hosting platforms, computational/storage requirements, and deployment models are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with Building Information Modeling (BIM) standards and external systems through bidirectional data exchange, addressing interoperability across heterogeneous systems as part of non-functional requirements; specific data exchange protocols and schemas are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework utilizes BIM as an AECO-specific standard for device modeling but does not explicitly reference additional industry standards, specifications, or interoperability protocols. Compliance frameworks and certification requirements are not detailed in the documents."
]
